memberdef.argsstring: (const Base< pod_type, T1 > &A, const Base< pod_type, T2 > &B)
memberdef.name: fixed

detaileddescription.para: print contents of the matrix (to the cout stream), optionally preceding with a user specified line of text. the precision and cell width are modified. on return, the stream's state are restored to their original values.

print contents of the matrix to a user specified stream, optionally preceding with a user specified line of text. the precision and cell width are modified. on return, the stream's state are restored to their original values.

briefdescription.para: DEPRECATED FUNCTION.
DEPRECATED FUNCTION.

detaileddescription.para: print contents of the matrix (to the cout stream), optionally preceding with a user specified line of text. the stream's state are used as is and are not modified (i.e. the precision and cell width are not modified).

print contents of the matrix to a user specified stream, optionally preceding with a user specified line of text. the stream's state are used as is and are not modified. (i.e. the precision and cell width are not modified).

briefdescription.para: DEPRECATED FUNCTION.
DEPRECATED FUNCTION.
returns true if the given index is currently in range
returns true if the given start and end indices are currently in range

memberdef.argsstring: (const uword in_row, const uword in_col) const
memberdef.name: in_range

briefdescription.para: internal matrix construction; if the requested size is small enough, memory from the stack is used. otherwise memory is allocated via 'new'

memberdef.argsstring: (const uword col_num, const uword N, const bool set_to_zero=true)
memberdef.name: insert_cols

detaileddescription.para: insert N columns at the specified column position, optionally setting the elements of the inserted columns to zero

memberdef.name: insert_cols

detaileddescription.para: insert the given object at the specified column position; the given object must have the same number of rows as the matrix

memberdef.argsstring: (const uword row_num, const uword N, const bool set_to_zero=true)
memberdef.name: insert_rows

detaileddescription.para: insert N rows at the specified row position, optionally setting the elements of the inserted rows to zero

detaileddescription.para: insert the given object at the specified row position; the given object must have the same number of columns as the matrix

briefdescription.para: returns true if the object can be interpreted as a column vector
returns true if the matrix has no elements
returns true if all of the elements are finite
returns true if the object can be interpreted as a row vector
returns true if the object has the same number of non-zero rows and columnns
returns true if the object can be interpreted as a column or row vector

memberdef.argsstring: (eT *aux_mem, const uword aux_n_rows, const uword aux_n_cols, const bool copy_aux_mem=true, const bool strict=true)
memberdef.name: Mat

detaileddescription.para: construct a matrix from a given auxiliary array of eTs. if copy_aux_mem is true, new memory is allocated and the array is copied. if copy_aux_mem is false, the auxiliary array is used directly (without allocating memory and copying). the default is to copy the array.

memberdef.argsstring: (const eT *aux_mem, const uword aux_n_rows, const uword aux_n_cols)
memberdef.name: Mat

detaileddescription.para: construct a matrix from a given auxiliary read-only array of eTs. the array is copied.

briefdescription.para: construct a matrix from a given matrix
construct a matrix from subview (e.g. construct a matrix from a delayed submatrix operation)
construct a matrix from a subview_cube instance
construct a matrix from diagview (e.g. construct a matrix from a delayed diag operation)

detaileddescription.para: create a Col object which uses memory from an existing matrix object. this approach is currently not alias safe and does not take into account that the parent matrix object could be deleted. if deleted memory is accessed by the created Col object, it will cause memory corruption and/or a crash

create a Col object which uses memory from an existing matrix object. this approach is currently not alias safe and does not take into account that the parent matrix object could be deleted. if deleted memory is accessed by the created Col object, it will cause memory corruption and/or a crash

memberdef.name: zeros
